text: When you write code, encountering bugs is normal. Identifying bugs can be challenging, so it's helpful to take a systematic approach. First, try to reproduce the error and observe its behavior. Use debugging tools like breakpoints to pause the code and inspect variables step by step. Logging is also very useful — by adding print statements or log messages, you can track how data flows through your program. This gives you a clear picture of where things go wrong. A systematic method saves time and reduces frustration. Remember, debugging is a skill you improve with practice.
